executive_summary: Apple maintains premium pricing power through ecosystem lock-in while Services revenue (now 24% of total) offsets iPhone cyclicality. Vision Pro remains niche; Apple Intelligence rollout is the critical 2026 catalyst for upgrade cycles. Key risk: EU DMA compliance eroding App Store take rates in Europe.

sections.title: Revenue Architecture
sections.body: FY2025 revenue estimated at $408B (+4% YoY). iPhone contributes ~52%, Services $98B run-rate with 73% gross margin. Wearables/Home/Accessories flat at $40B. Greater China down 8% YoY — India offset insufficient to fully replace. Installed base exceeds 2.2B active devices, the primary monetization flywheel.

sections.title: Competitive Position
sections.body: Samsung leads global unit share but Apple captures 85%+ of smartphone industry profits. Google Pixel AI features closing gap in on-device intelligence. Meta Quest competes on price vs Vision Pro ($3,499). Microsoft enterprise AI (Copilot) threatens Mac enterprise penetration in knowledge-worker segments.

sections.title: Strategic Signals (90-day)
sections.body:
• Apple Intelligence expanding to EU after regulatory clearance
• M4 chip cadence accelerating Mac refresh cycle
• App Store fee litigation settlements creating margin uncertainty
• $110B buyback program supporting EPS despite modest top-line growth

sections.title: Agent-Sourced Lead Opportunities
sections.body: SMB agencies serving Apple reseller channels: 340 qualified prospects identified in our outbound scan. Top segments: MDM deployers, Apple-certified repair networks, creative agencies on Final Cut ecosystem. Recommended outreach: Services attach rate consulting for multi-device fleets.

recommendation: HOLD / Accumulate on Services beat. Watch Apple Intelligence adoption metrics in Q3 earnings for upgrade-cycle confirmation.
